title: Express 5 Routing, Path Syntax, Parameters, and Dispatch · Inference Brief: Express5 Path Syntax
summary: An Express 4 route table is moved unchanged, wildcard values change shape, optional syntax breaks, route order shadows literals, and decoded parameters are trusted as identifiers. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts express5 path syntax increment in Express 5 route grammar and dispatch table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author Express 5.2 string paths with named wildcards, braces, reserved characters, and supported path-to-regexp syntax.
Require live TLS identity, proxy, slow-client, protocol negotiation, upgrade, drain, and packet-level evidence beyond deterministic browser models.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Express5 Path Syntax · Theory Express5 Path Syntax
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6762 in a document service receiving slow compressed uploads. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to preserve Express 5 route order and named wildcard-array evidence; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Express5 Path Syntax contract for the supplied data.
